Apple Inc. 10-K Annual Report — Fiscal Year 2024
United States Securities and Exchange Commission
Washington, D.C. 20549

ITEM 1. BUSINESS

Apple Inc. (the "Company," "Apple") designs, manufactures, and markets smartphones, personal computers, tablets, wearables, and accessories. Apple also sells a variety of related services. The Company's fiscal year is the 52 or 53-week period that ends on the last Saturday of September.

Apple is committed to bringing the best user experience to its customers through its innovative hardware, software, and services. Apple's products and services include iPh

s through its innovative hardware, software, and services. Apple's products and services include iPhone, Mac, iPad, Apple Watch, AirPods, Apple TV, HomePod, and a portfolio of consumer and professional software applications.

PRODUCTS

iPhone is Apple's line of smartphones based on its iOS operating system. iPhone designs incorporate motion sensors, cameras, displays, and cellular network capabilities. In fiscal 2024, iPhone net sales were $201.0 billion, representing approximately 52% of total net sales, compared to $205.5 billion in fiscal 2023.

Mac includes desktops and notebooks based on

net sales, compared to $205.5 billion in fiscal 2023.

Mac includes desktops and notebooks based on Apple silicon. Mac net sales were $29.8 billion in fiscal 2024, compared to $29.4 billion in fiscal 2023.

iPad includes iPad Pro, iPad Air, iPad, and iPad mini. iPad net sales were $26.7 billion in fiscal 2024, compared to $28.3 billion in fiscal 2023.

Wearables, Home and Accessories includes Apple Watch, AirPods, Beats products, Apple TV, and accessories. Net sales were $37.0 billion in fiscal 2024, compared to $39.8 billion in fiscal 2023.

Services include advertising, AppleCare, cloud serv

2024, compared to $39.8 billion in fiscal 2023.

Services include advertising, AppleCare, cloud services, digital content, payment, and licensing. Services net sales were $96.2 billion in fiscal 2024, compared to $85.2 billion in fiscal 2023 — a growth of approximately 13%.

ITEM 1A. RISK FACTORS

The following discussion of risk factors contains forward-looking statements. These risk factors may be important to understanding any statement in this Form 10-K or elsewhere.

Macroeconomic Risks: The Company's business and financial condition are sensitive to global and regional macroeconomic cond

e Company's business and financial condition are sensitive to global and regional macroeconomic conditions, including inflation, interest rates, and consumer spending trends. Uncertain or weak economic conditions may harm the Company's business and results of operations.

Competition Risk: The markets for the Company's products and services are highly competitive, and the Company is subject to intense competition in all areas of its business. The Company faces competition from vertically integrated competitors that may have better access to key components, lower cost structures, or alternative

ted competitors that may have better access to key components, lower cost structures, or alternative revenue models.

Supply Chain Risk: The Company has outsourced manufacturing to third parties. Global supply chain disruptions, including those arising from natural disasters, geopolitical tensions, or pandemics, could adversely impact production and availability of the Company's products.

Intellectual Property Risk: The Company could be found to have infringed the intellectual property rights of others. The Company regularly receives notices from third parties alleging patent or other intelle

f others. The Company regularly receives notices from third parties alleging patent or other intellectual property infringement.

Regulatory Risk: The Company is subject to complex and changing laws and regulations worldwide, including those related to privacy, data protection, content, antitrust, and tax. Compliance with these requirements creates significant costs and may reduce the Company's flexibility.

ITEM 7. MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS

Fiscal Year 2024 Highlights

Total net sales for fiscal 2024 were $391.0 billion, compared to $383.3 billion in fiscal 2023, an increase of ap

es for fiscal 2024 were $391.0 billion, compared to $383.3 billion in fiscal 2023, an increase of approximately 2%. This growth was primarily driven by strong performance in our Services segment.

Total operating expenses for fiscal 2024 were $53.9 billion, compared to $54.8 billion in fiscal 2023.

Net income for fiscal 2024 was $93.7 billion, compared to $97.0 billion in fiscal 2023. Diluted earnings per share were $6.08, compared to $6.13 in fiscal 2023.

Gross margin for fiscal 2024 was $180.7 billion or 46.2%, compared to $169.1 billion or 44.1% in fiscal 2023. The increase in gross margi

.7 billion or 46.2%, compared to $169.1 billion or 44.1% in fiscal 2023. The increase in gross margin percentage was primarily due to an improved mix driven by Services growth and favorable product mix.

LIQUIDITY AND CAPITAL RESOURCES

The Company believes its existing balances of cash and cash equivalents and short-term investments, together with cash generated by operations and access to credit markets, will be sufficient to satisfy its cash requirements and capital return program over the next twelve months.

Cash and cash equivalents and current and non-current marketable securities total

xt twelve months.

Cash and cash equivalents and current and non-current marketable securities totaled $153.2 billion as of September 28, 2024, compared to $162.1 billion as of September 30, 2023.

During fiscal 2024, the Company returned $110.0 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. The Company also paid dividends of $0.96 per share during fiscal 2024.

ITEM 1A. ADDITIONAL RISK FACTORS

Cybersecurity Risk: The Company's products, services, and business operations are subject to cybersecurity threats including malware, ransomware, and sophisticated state-sponsored attacks that could disrupt operations or expose sensitive customer data.

Geopolitical Risk: The Company's operations in China are subject to significant political, regulatory, and economic risks. China represents approximately 17% of net sales. Restrictions on trade, travel bans, or regulatory actions by Chinese authorities could materially im

Restrictions on trade, travel bans, or regulatory actions by Chinese authorities could materially impact revenues.

ITEM 13. EXECUTIVE COMPENSATION

Total compensation for CEO Timothy D. Cook in fiscal 2024 was approximately $74.6 million, consisting of base salary of $3.0 million, annual cash incentive of $8.1 million, and long-term equity awards of approximately $63.5 million.

Tesla, Inc. 10-K Annual Report — Fiscal Year 2024
United States Securities and Exchange Commission

ITEM 1. BUSINESS

Tesla, Inc. designs, develops, manufactures, leases, and sells electric vehicles, energy generation and storage systems, and related products and services. The Company also offers vehicle service, charging infrastructure, and full self-driving capability software. Tesla was incorporated in Delaware in July 2003.

Tesla's mission is to accelerate the world's transition to sustainable energy. To achieve this mission, the Company has designed and deployed a vertically integrated e

ble energy. To achieve this mission, the Company has designed and deployed a vertically integrated energy and transportation ecosystem.

AUTOMOTIVE SEGMENT

The Company's automotive segment includes the design, development, manufacturing, leasing, and sale of electric vehicles. The Company currently manufactures the Model 3, Model Y, Model S, Model X, and Cybertruck. Model Y is the Company's highest volume vehicle.

In fiscal year 2024, total automotive revenues were $77.1 billion, compared to $82.4 billion in fiscal year 2023, a decrease of approximately 6%. The decrease was primarily driven

82.4 billion in fiscal year 2023, a decrease of approximately 6%. The decrease was primarily driven by average selling price reductions as the Company prioritized volume and expanded into new markets.

The Company delivered approximately 1,789,226 vehicles in fiscal year 2024, compared to 1,808,581 deliveries in fiscal year 2023.

ENERGY GENERATION AND STORAGE SEGMENT

The Energy Generation and Storage segment includes the design, manufacture, installation, sale, and leasing of solar energy systems, Powerwall, Megapack, and related products. Energy revenues were $10.1 billion in fiscal 2024, c

ems, Powerwall, Megapack, and related products. Energy revenues were $10.1 billion in fiscal 2024, compared to $6.0 billion in fiscal 2023, representing 68% growth driven primarily by Megapack deployments.

SERVICES AND OTHER

Services and Other revenues were $10.1 billion in fiscal 2024, compared to $8.3 billion in fiscal 2023. This segment includes non-warranty after-sales vehicle services, body shop and parts, paid Supercharging, retail merchandise, and insurance.

ITEM 1A. RISK FACTORS

Competition Risk: The electric vehicle market is intensely competitive, with significant and well-resour

etition Risk: The electric vehicle market is intensely competitive, with significant and well-resourced competitors including traditional automotive companies as well as new EV-focused entrants. BYD, Volkswagen Group, General Motors, Ford, and Hyundai/Kia have all significantly increased their EV production capacity and market share.

Pricing Pressure Risk: The Company has reduced vehicle prices significantly over recent periods. Continued pricing pressure from competitors may require further reductions, compressing automotive gross margins. In fiscal 2024, automotive gross margin excluding re

ductions, compressing automotive gross margins. In fiscal 2024, automotive gross margin excluding regulatory credits was approximately 14.6%.

Autonomous Driving Risk: The Company's Full Self-Driving capabilities are subject to extensive regulatory scrutiny and have been involved in several investigations by the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA). Adverse regulatory decisions could require product recalls or curtail deployment.

Execution Risk on New Products: The Company's growth depends substantially on successful development and production of next-generation vehicles, in

rowth depends substantially on successful development and production of next-generation vehicles, including the planned lower-cost compact vehicle expected in 2025, and the Robotaxi. Delays or cost overruns would adversely impact financial results.

Energy Cost Risk: The Company's Gigafactories consume substantial electricity. Increases in electricity costs or restrictions on energy availability could increase manufacturing costs. The Company operates Gigafactories in Austin (Texas), Fremont (California), Berlin (Germany), and Shanghai (China).

Geopolitical and Trade Risk: Approximately 23% o

lifornia), Berlin (Germany), and Shanghai (China).

Geopolitical and Trade Risk: Approximately 23% of the Company's revenues originated in China in fiscal 2024. Tariffs, trade restrictions, or regulatory actions by Chinese authorities could significantly impair the Company's operations and financial results.

ITEM 7. MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS

Total revenues for fiscal year 2024 were $97.7 billion, compared to $96.8 billion in fiscal year 2023, an increase of approximately 1%. Total gross profit was $17.8 billion in fiscal 2024, representing a gross margin of 18.2%, compared to a gr

ross profit was $17.8 billion in fiscal 2024, representing a gross margin of 18.2%, compared to a gross margin of 18.2% in fiscal 2023.

Total operating expenses were $11.6 billion in fiscal 2024, compared to $8.8 billion in fiscal 2023, primarily due to increased research and development expenses related to next-generation vehicle development and AI/robotics initiatives.

Net income attributable to common stockholders was $7.1 billion in fiscal 2024, compared to $15.0 billion in fiscal 2023. The decrease in net income was primarily driven by lower automotive margins, higher operating expenses

e decrease in net income was primarily driven by lower automotive margins, higher operating expenses, and reduced interest income. Diluted earnings per share were $2.04 in fiscal 2024, compared to $4.30 in fiscal 2023.

Research and development expenses were $4.5 billion in fiscal 2024, compared to $3.97 billion in fiscal 2023, primarily related to development of the next generation vehicle platform, Full Self-Driving capabilities, the Optimus humanoid robot, and AI infrastructure including the Dojo supercomputer.

SUPERCHARGER NETWORK

As of December 31, 2024, the Company operated over 6,500 Supercharger stations globally with over 63,000 connectors, making it the world's largest fast-charging network. Tesla has opened its Supercharger network to other electric vehicle brands in North America and Europe.

la has opened its Supercharger network to other electric vehicle brands in North America and Europe.

AUTOPILOT AND FULL SELF-DRIVING

As of December 31, 2024, the Company had accumulated more than 5 billion miles of Autopilot data. Full Self-Driving (Supervised) capability is available through a subscription at $99/month or a one-time purchase of $8,000. The Company is working toward deploying an unsupervised FSD capability and launching a Robotaxi service.

Microsoft Corporation 10-K Annual Report — Fiscal Year 2024
United States Securities and Exchange Commission
Washington, D.C. 20549

ITEM 1. BUSINESS

Microsoft Corporation enables digital transformation for the era of an intelligent cloud and an intelligent edge. Microsoft develops and supports a wide range of software products, services, devices, and solutions. The Company's fiscal year ends June 30.

Microsoft's segments are: Productivity and Business Processes, Intelligent Cloud, and More Personal Computing.

PRODUCTIVITY AND BUSINESS PROCESSES

This segment includes Microsoft 365 (formerl

rsonal Computing.

PRODUCTIVITY AND BUSINESS PROCESSES

This segment includes Microsoft 365 (formerly Office 365), Exchange, SharePoint, Microsoft Teams, LinkedIn, and Dynamics 365. Segment revenues were $77.5 billion in fiscal 2024, compared to $69.3 billion in fiscal 2023, an increase of 12%.

Microsoft 365 Commercial cloud revenue grew 15% year-over-year, driven by continued expansion into enterprise customers and the integration of Copilot AI capabilities. Microsoft Teams has over 320 million monthly active users. LinkedIn revenue grew 10% with continued strength in Talent Solutions and Ma

n monthly active users. LinkedIn revenue grew 10% with continued strength in Talent Solutions and Marketing Solutions.

Dynamics 365, Microsoft's cloud-based ERP and CRM suite, grew 19% in fiscal 2024. The integration of Copilot AI assistant functionality across Dynamics 365 applications has been a significant growth driver.

INTELLIGENT CLOUD

This segment includes Azure, Windows Server, SQL Server, and GitHub. Segment revenues were $105.4 billion in fiscal 2024, compared to $87.9 billion in fiscal 2023, an increase of 20%.

Azure and other cloud services grew 29% in fiscal 2024, accelerating

scal 2023, an increase of 20%.

Azure and other cloud services grew 29% in fiscal 2024, accelerating from 27% growth in fiscal 2023. Azure AI services have become a critical growth driver, as organizations adopt generative AI capabilities. Microsoft's partnership with OpenAI provides exclusive access to GPT-4 class models through Azure OpenAI Service, which has seen rapid enterprise adoption.

GitHub, acquired in 2018, reached 100 million developers in fiscal 2024. GitHub Copilot, the AI-powered coding assistant, has over 1.8 million paid subscribers across enterprise and individual plans.

MO

coding assistant, has over 1.8 million paid subscribers across enterprise and individual plans.

MORE PERSONAL COMPUTING

This segment includes Windows OEM, Devices (Surface), Gaming (Xbox), and Search/News advertising (Bing). Segment revenues were $62.3 billion in fiscal 2024, compared to $54.7 billion in fiscal 2023, an increase of 14%.

Gaming revenues grew 61% primarily due to the acquisition of Activision Blizzard, which was completed in October 2023 for approximately $75.4 billion. Xbox Game Pass has approximately 34 million subscribers.

Search and news advertising revenues grew 12%, d

ame Pass has approximately 34 million subscribers.

Search and news advertising revenues grew 12%, driven by AI-enhanced Bing and Edge market share gains following the integration of Copilot search capabilities.

ITEM 1A. RISK FACTORS

AI and Competitive Risk: The Company has made substantial investments in artificial intelligence, including its partnership with OpenAI and integration of Copilot features across products. There is no assurance these investments will generate expected returns. Competitors including Google, Amazon, Meta, and Apple are also investing heavily in AI capabilities. Fa

petitors including Google, Amazon, Meta, and Apple are also investing heavily in AI capabilities. Failure to execute on AI strategy could result in market share loss and revenue underperformance.

Cybersecurity Risk: In January 2024, the Company disclosed that a Russian state-sponsored threat actor known as Midnight Blizzard gained unauthorized access to a small percentage of Microsoft corporate email accounts. The Company has undertaken significant remediation efforts. Future cybersecurity incidents could damage the Company's reputation and result in significant financial loss.

Regulatory Ri

dents could damage the Company's reputation and result in significant financial loss.

Regulatory Risk: The Company is subject to increasing regulatory scrutiny globally, including competition law investigations in Europe and the United States related to its cloud computing and AI practices. The Digital Markets Act in the European Union imposes obligations on designated gatekeepers. The Company's Activision Blizzard acquisition faced significant regulatory review.

OpenAI Partnership Risk: The Company has invested approximately $13 billion in OpenAI and has an exclusive cloud partnership. Chan

ompany has invested approximately $13 billion in OpenAI and has an exclusive cloud partnership. Changes in the OpenAI partnership, including governance changes or competitive responses from OpenAI, could adversely impact the Company's AI strategy and Azure growth.

Economic Sensitivity: A meaningful portion of the Company's revenue is derived from enterprise software subscriptions and cloud services. An economic downturn could cause customers to reduce IT spending, impacting renewal rates and new customer acquisition.

ITEM 7. MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS

Fiscal Year 2024 Highlights

w customer acquisition.

ITEM 7. MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS

Fiscal Year 2024 Highlights

Total revenue for fiscal 2024 was $245.1 billion, compared to $211.9 billion in fiscal 2023, an increase of 16%. This growth was driven by strong performance in Intelligent Cloud and continued strength in Productivity and Business Processes.

Total gross margin was $171.0 billion in fiscal 2024, representing a gross margin of 69.8%, compared to $146.1 billion or 68.9% in fiscal 2023. The improvement in gross margin was primarily driven by the higher mix of cloud services revenue and economies of

nt in gross margin was primarily driven by the higher mix of cloud services revenue and economies of scale in cloud infrastructure.

Operating income was $109.4 billion in fiscal 2024, compared to $88.5 billion in fiscal 2023, an increase of 24%. Operating margin expanded to 44.6% from 41.8% in fiscal 2023.

Net income for fiscal 2024 was $88.1 billion, compared to $72.4 billion in fiscal 2023, an increase of 22%. Diluted earnings per share were $11.80, compared to $9.65 in fiscal 2023.

Research and development expenses were $29.5 billion in fiscal 2024, compared to $27.2 billion in fiscal 20

h and development expenses were $29.5 billion in fiscal 2024, compared to $27.2 billion in fiscal 2023, reflecting continued investment in AI, cloud infrastructure, and next-generation product capabilities.

CAPITAL RETURN PROGRAM

During fiscal 2024, the Company returned $34.6 billion to shareholders through share repurchases and dividends. The Company paid dividends of $3.00 per share in fiscal 2024. The Board of Directors approved a new share repurchase program of $60 billion in September 2023.

AZURE AND CLOUD SERVICES

Azure infrastructure includes more than 60 announced regions globally, with 33 announced to come, which is more than any other cloud provider. Microsoft AI supercomputing infrastructure powers OpenAI training a

e than any other cloud provider. Microsoft AI supercomputing infrastructure powers OpenAI training and inference at scale, as well as Microsoft's own Copilot services.

Capital expenditures for fiscal 2024 were $55.7 billion, primarily for cloud and AI infrastructure including data center construction, servers, and networking equipment. The Company expects to continue significant capital investment to support AI and cloud growth.

COPILOT ECOSYSTEM

Microsoft 365 Copilot, launched broadly in November 2023, is available to enterprise customers at $30 per user per month. Early adoption has been

ember 2023, is available to enterprise customers at $30 per user per month. Early adoption has been strong among enterprise customers. Copilot for Microsoft 365 integrates GPT-4 and DALL-E models across Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, and Teams. GitHub Copilot Business is priced at $19 per user per month.

ITEM 13. EXECUTIVE COMPENSATION

Satya Nadella, Chairman and CEO, received total compensation of approximately $79.1 million in fiscal 2024, including base salary of $2.5 million, cash bonus of $10.7 million, and stock awards of $66.0 million.
